jobs.description:
Join the Amazon Checkout Experience team and help shape how millions of customers complete their purchases every day. You'll design and build scalable, high-performance systems that power our Address & Checkout services, solving complex technical challenges while directly improving the shopping experience at scale.

Key job responsibilities
- Design and develop full-stack solutions for Address & Checkout services, considering scalability, performance, and reliability across multi-tiered distributed systems
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams including product, design, and operations to understand customer needs and translate them into technical implementations
- Investigate and optimize system performance, latency, and resource utilization through data-driven analysis and experimentation
- Contribute to code quality and system testability by establishing best practices, conducting thorough code reviews, and identifying areas for improvement
- Communicate technical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, and participate actively in design and architecture discussions

A day in the life
As a Software Development Engineer on our team, you'll work on full-stack solutions that handle massive transaction volumes while maintaining lightning-fast response times. Your day might include collaborating with product and design partners to understand customer pain points in the checkout flow, writing and reviewing code for distributed systems that process millions of requests, investigating performance bottlenecks to optimize latency, and participating in design discussions with engineers across our organization. You'll balance building new features with strengthening the foundational systems that keep our services reliable and resilient. Beyond coding, you'll contribute to our culture of operational excellence by identifying opportunities to improve system quality, testability, and long-term health.

About the team
We're a collaborative group of engineers passionate about delivering seamless checkout experiences for Amazon customers worldwide. Our team focuses on building the infrastructure and services that make purchasing simple and dependable, even during peak shopping periods. We work closely with engineers at all levels across the organization, learning from one another and sharing knowledge to solve complex distributed systems challenges. Together, we're committed to continuously improving how customers interact with our platform, and we're excited to have someone join us who thinks deeply about both technical excellence and customer impact.

jobs.description:
Join our dynamic AWS team and become a critical guardian of global cloud infrastructure! You'll play a pivotal role in maintaining the heartbeat of the world's most innovative technology platform, ensuring seamless data center operations that power millions of businesses and services worldwide.

Data Center Technicians should be willing to work both independently and with a team. Work prioritization, organizational skills, effective communication, and the ability to react quickly are critical to success. In addition to hardware and network repair, candidates will install equipment, create documentation, innovate solutions, and fix problems within the data center space. This team works in an environment that operates 24/7. Traveling within and outside of the regional work area is required.

AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

NOTE: Amazon does not sponsor for immigration, including for H-1B, TN, and other non-immigrant visas, for this role.

NOTE: Lump sum stipend will be provided to eligible candidates who relocate for this position.

NOTE: This position requires the successful candidate report to a site location. This position is in-person and on-site. The candidate will work in an environment that operates 24/7.

Key job responsibilities
- Performing server rack installations, hardware break-fix on various components, troubleshooting network issues, and responding to operational incidents that impact service availability, escalating to senior technicians and management as needed/required
- Serving as a primary contact point for both internal and external stakeholders, including engineers, software developers, vendors, and contractors
- Performing on-call duties and participating in scheduled maintenance and change management activities
- Contributing to documentation and process improvement initiatives based on your analysis of operational issues
- Helping to train and onboard new team members

Physical requirements:
- Regularly lift and/or move up to 40 pounds; and participate in group lifts for 41+ pounds
- Working in cramped and/or elevated locations
- Bending, lifting, stretching, and reaching
- Standing and walking for up to 8+ hours a day
- Ascending and descending ladders, stairs, and gangways safely and without limitation
- Work in a noisy environment
- Work shifts longer than 8 hours to support 24/7 operations (covering both night and day shifts).

This role supports data centers in various operational stages. The position may be assigned to a facility still under development, which could require travel to established locations for training until your designated site becomes fully operational.

Essential Requirements:
- Ability to travel to or commute between data center locations as needed
- Willingness to temporarily work at alternative sites during training periods or until assigned facility is operational
- Travel frequency will vary based on business needs and operational status of assigned facility

Candidates must be able to travel (over 50 miles) or commute (less than 50 miles) to active data center sites as required by business needs.

A day in the life
As a Data Center Technician professional, you have industry-leading technical abilities and demonstrate a breadth of knowledge while you:
- Take ownership of technical issues brought by their customer base, engaging other teams when needed to drive resolution
- Solve problems at their root and step back to understand the broader context
- Maintain service level agreements through the implementation of proactive issue detection and reporting

You will be required to work shift work that will include days/nights/weekends/holidays. Traveling within and outside of the regional work area is required. You will be responsible for having a reliable personal vehicle and valid driver's license to travel within the regional work area, as company transportation will not be provided.

jobs.description:
Our WW Operations network delivers millions of packages and smiles to Amazon customers every day. We are looking for motivated, customer-focused individuals who want to join our team as an Area Manager. In this role, you will lead a team of hourly workforce and will be responsible for engaging your team during their shifts to maintain the highest levels of safety, quality, attendance, and performance. You will also play a key role in maintaining our customer expectations to ensure customer orders are delivered at the right time, to the right location.

Our Operation’s workflow has three major components: First mile - where the product is housed and ready for order; Middle mile - where the order is hauled to your area; and Last mile - when the product is delivered to the customer’s door. Please note that all workflows have slight building variations, but one thing is constant: our vision and dedication to the customer.

Key Responsibilities and Job Elements:
- Support, mentor, and motivate your hourly workforce
- Manage safety, quality, productivity, and customer delivery promises
- Collaborate with all support teams including Safety, Engineering, Loss Prevention, Quality Assurance, Human Resources to develop plans to meet business objectives
- Lift up to 49 pounds and frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach
- Stand/walk for up to 12 hours during shifts
- Work in an environment where the noise level varies and can be loud
- Work in an environment that is subject to variable temperatures and weather (delivery stations include outside loading departments)
- Continuously climb and descend stairs (applies to sites with stairs)
Our fulfillment network launches new Operations sites every year, providing various opportunities for your professional growth. We hire Area Managers based on location preference and the business’ current openings.
jobs.description_short: Our WW Operations network delivers millions of packages and smiles to Amazon customers every day. We are looking for motivated, customer-focused individuals who want to join our team as an Area Manager.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- スタートアップ、ベンチャーキャピタル、または技術職に焦点を当てたテクノロジー業界での4年以上の経験
- クラウドコンピューティングアーキテクチャ、サービス、ベストプラクティスに関する深い理解
- スタートアップの文脈における技術ソリューションの評価経験
- 戦略的ビジネスイニシアチブの実行実績
- 技術分野の学士号または同等の経験
jobs.description:
技術的専門知識と戦略的ビジネス開発を組み合わせて、ベンチャーキャピタルとその投資先スタートアップ企業に対するAWSのエンゲージメントを推進してみませんか？またはベンチャーキャピタル投資を受けたスタートアップの基盤としてAWSを確立するための技術的知識とビジネス洞察力をお持ちですか？

世界中のより多くのスタートアップから信頼されているAWSは、クラウドコンピューティングの力をすべての人にアクセス可能にしています。私たちは、世界最大級の企業を支えているテクノロジーへのアクセスを、世界中のスタートアップ創業者に提供しています。AWSスタートアップチームは、数十万のスタートアップを支援してきた約20年の経験を活かし、成長段階や資金調達レベルに関わらずスタートアップ創業者が世界を変えるアイデアの実現にむけた支援しています。現在多くのスタートアップ、そしてユニコーンの80%以上がAWS上でのローンチを選択する背景です。

AWS内のシニアインベストメントマネージャーとして、ベンチャーキャピタルとその投資先ポートフォリオ企業との複雑かつ戦略的なイニシアチブを担当します。クラウドテクノロジーに関する技術的理解とビジネス開発の経験を活用して、高い可能性を持つスタートアップとのエンゲージメントを評価、サポート、成長させます。

Key job responsibilities
- 担当するアーリーステージ特化のベンチャーキャピタルおよびシードファンドのポートフォリオにおいて、AWSの市場戦略および技術戦略を実行
- スタートアップのアーキテクチャと技術スタックを理解することで、技術的およびビジネス的可能性を評価
- ベンチャー企業との関係を構築・維持し、AWSの機能に関するトレーニングを提供
- AWSサービスとスタートアップのニーズを整合させる戦略的イニシアチブを実施
- ポートフォリオ企業に対し、クラウドアーキテクチャおよび市場参入戦略に関する技術的・ビジネス的ガイダンスを提供
- スタートアップの成長を支援するプログラムを実行するため、部門横断チームを調整
- ポートフォリオの進捗と課題に関するビジネスレビューを作成・提供

jobs.description:
Amazon Advertising is dedicated to drive measurable outcomes for brand advertisers, agencies, authors, and entrepreneurs. Our ad solutions, including sponsored ads, display, video, and custom ads, leverage Amazon’s innovations and insights to find, attract, and engage intended audiences throughout their daily journeys. With a range of flexible pricing and buying models, including selfservice, managed service, and programmatic ad buying, these solutions help businesses to build brand awareness, increase product sales, and more.

Key job responsibilities
Deliver the revenue goals through exploring the new business opportunities through identifying and creating new relationships with advertisers.
Understand Amazon's search and display advertising tools to help build relevant and effective advertising solutions for our advertisers.
Understand and learn about the e-commerce landscape and competitive environment, including knowledge of competitive product offerings.
Deliver the highest level of educational and consultant services to China-based brands and advertisers.
Help advertisers see how to best utilize Amazon’s ad tools in order to better build their business.
Coordinate campaign setup with the global advertising teams.
Dive deep on campaign performance reports and work with advertising teams around the global to enable optimal performance to advertisers in each locale.

jobs.description:
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is looking for a Marketing Operations intern for the GCR Marketing organization. This role will support business planning, reporting, analysis and operations for GCR Marketing. You will be responsible for designing and managing the reporting, work closely with the GCR Marketing, and the global team to establish report structure, drive operational cadence and process improvement, and deliver analytical insights.

This role requires an individual with excellent analytical abilities, outstanding business acumen, and exceptional written and oral communication skills. The successful candidate will be data-driven and has an understanding of Marketing KPIs and go-to-market channels. The candidate will be adept at building models, reports and tools with a deep understanding of the underlying analytics, and the ability to assimilate new methodologies and data sources. The individual will be a self-starter who is comfortable with ambiguity, possesses strong attention to detail, and an ability to work in a fast-paced and ever-changing environment.


Key job responsibilities
• Work with the GCR Marketing and Global Lead Management teams to manage and optimize the reporting structure.
• Recommend metrics and build reports in Salesforce or Tableau to quantify business impact from GCR Marketing activities.
• Build enablement capabilities and drive data adoption.
• Provide weekly and monthly reports, trends and insights.
• Prepare regular business reviews within GCR Marketing and contribute actively in global reviews.

jobs.description:
Are you interested in building high-performance, globally scalable Financial systems and infrastructure that support Amazon's current and future growth? Are you seeking an environment where you can drive innovation? Does the prospect of working with top finance and engineering talent get you charged up? If so, Amazon Finance Technology (FinTech) is for you!

Amazon FinTech is looking for a Senior Product Manager Technical (PM-T) to deliver long term scalable Project Financial Subledger that will help our Accounting and Finance customers improve financial reporting for Construction and Digital businesses, accelerate financial close cycle, enhance controllership and reduce manual effort through automation.

We are seeking an experienced, results-oriented, self-motivated Technical Product Manager, having a bent for solving complex problems and delivering high quality technical products. You will be responsible for developing product strategy, defining product requirements working with global internal accounting teams, influencing stakeholders and engineering teams required to deliver an impactful product.

The ideal candidate for this role will have strong ownership, business and product sense combined with good understanding of AI and ML applications, specific use cases, enjoy diving deep on complex processes, engaging with senior leaders to drive change, be an educator who inspires by sharing knowledge in an accessible way, and finally collaborate cross-functionally to deliver sustainable results.
You will need to thrive and succeed in an entrepreneurial environment without being hindered by ambiguity. High judgment, ability to influence, analytical talent, ability to partner with technical and non-technical stakeholders and leadership, data and doc writing, have strong attention to detail and polished communication skills are essential to success in this role.

Key job responsibilities
- Define product requirements and execution strategy of AI applications, which matches our overall strategy

- Lead the product life cycle including vision, strategy, roadmap, requirements, user experience, delivery, launch, and support.

- Work with engineering, research, UX, technical program managers and stakeholders on driving initiatives and delivering a successful product

- Collaborate with Amazon partner tech teams to ensure product re-usability and avoid redundancies.

- Managing both upwards and downwards communications effectively

A day in the life
- Create an integrated and prioritized intake mechanism
- Increase product adoption across finance, tax and accounting through office hours, demos and trainings.
- Write BRDs and business use cases.
- Experiment and explore novel AI solutions by developing Proof of Concept ideas that can be scaled into full-fledged applications.
- Participate and influences the central AI initiatives by being the voice of customer in these discussions.
- Join and shape leadership discussion through product vision
- Be a customer obsessed thought leader who dives deep on customer facing defects to find a scalable solution.


About the team
FinTech’s charter is to enable Finance & Global Business Services (FGBS) teams, and amplify the benefit of FGBS teams to support the growth, expansion, and restructuring of the businesses. The Close Systems team owns coordination, execution and continuous improvement for month close processes across FGBS teams. We own developing automation to scale with Amazon’s growth, reduce manual effort and defects, streamline processes, and drive accountability across teams. The Close Systems team improves the financial close by developing automation to scale with Amazon’s growth, reduce manual effort and defects, streamline processes, and drive accountability across teams.

jobs.description:
Imagine being at the forefront of revolutionizing the tax industry with technologies. As a Sr. Manager, Product Management – Technical in FinTech Tax Technology team, you will have the opportunity to shape the future of tax products and solutions that empower businesses and individuals to navigate the ever-evolving landscape of tax regulations and compliance requirements.
In this pivotal role, you will be responsible for driving the strategic vision and technical roadmap for our innovative tax product suite. You will lead a dynamic team of Product Managers, collaborating with cross-functional stakeholders to translate complex tax requirements into robust, scalable, and secure software solutions. Your expertise in product management will be instrumental in delivering products that not only meet but exceed customer expectations. As a thought leader in the tax technology domain, you will foster a culture of innovation and continuous improvement within your team. You will mentor and develop technical talent, encouraging them to push boundaries and embrace innovative approaches to solving complex tax challenges.

Join our team and be part of a dynamic, forward-thinking organization that values excellence, collaboration, and a passion for leveraging technology to simplify the complexities of tax.

Key job responsibilities
A successful candidate will have led product organization, built and scaled high performing technical product teams, be familiar with operational best practices, be dedicated to diversity and inclusivity, have world class business acumen to be stubborn about the long-term vision, flexible on the implementation details, and clever in driving short term, incremental results.

About the team
The Finance Technology Tax team builds technology to support Amazon’s global tax teams (our customers). Our customers need to ensure that Amazon is compliant with the increasingly complex tax regulations. We build systems to enable our customers to perform tax functions including, calculations, payments, filing, tax audits, and recovery at the scale demanded by Amazon’s business operations. We prioritize solutions based on our ability to deliver exponential value in the following four dimensions: 1) supporting business growth while reducing cost to serve 2) ensuring compliance and reducing risk 3) improving customer experience, and 4) providing accurate, complete, and timely data.

jobs.description:
本ポジションの勤務地は、兵庫県にある尼崎フルフィルメントセンターになります。

Amazonの入荷・出荷のプロセスを行うFCには、世界最先端の自動化物流設備が導入されています。コンベア、ロボット、自動仕分けシステムなど、最新テクノロジーを活用し、商品の入出荷を効率的に行っています。このような先進的な環境で、次世代の物流技術に携わることができます。
　
RME（Reliability and Maintenance Engineering）部門は、日々物流設備の保全と保守、改善を行い、お客様に満足いただけるように努めております。今回、RME部門が募集する職種はFCに設置されている入出荷オペレーションに関わる設備・装置を安全に取り扱い、安定稼働のための物流設備の軽微なエラー対応、および保全作業や設備の安全性や生産性を向上させるための改善を担当いただく「物流設備メンテナンスサポート」というポジションになります。
　
本ポジションは保全業務が未経験の方からのご応募を歓迎しており、主体的に学ぶ姿勢を持ち、保全エンジニアとしてのキャリアに強い意欲のある方を求めております。入社後は体系的な教育プログラムと、経験豊富な先輩社員によるサポートにより、実践的な実務経験と専門技術を身に付けていくことができます。
　
RMEにて技術力と現場感を身に着けることで将来的なキャリア選択肢は大きく広がります。RME内にてマネージャーや技術スペシャリストとして活躍することはもちろん、社内公募制度を利用して他エンジニアリングチーム（設備導入、設計、開発など）へ異動し活躍の幅を広げることも可能です。
　
【More Information】
■メンテナンス部門について
　・メンテナンスエンジニア特設サイト
　・社員インタビュー/堺FC勤務の社員T.S.さん
　・社員インタビュー/堺FC勤務の社員E.M.さん
■勤務地について
　・フルフィルメントセンター (FC)
　・勤務地:尼崎FC
　
Amazon は多様かつインクルーシブな職場づくりを目指しています。Amazonは男女雇用機会均等法を順守しています。人種、 出身国、性別、性的指向、障がい、年齢、その他の属性によって差別することなく、平等に採用選考の機会を提供しています。障がいをお持ちの方は、以下をご覧ください。https://www.amazon.jobs/disability/jp
オペレーション部門のDEIの取り組み

Key job responsibilities
【具体的な業務内容】
下記業務をチームで対応いただきます。
■60%：日常点検・異常対応…稼働監視/巡回による設備点検/異常対応、故障復旧/オーバーホール、部品修繕
■20%：メンテナンス業務…予防保全/定期保全/状態保全/事後保全
■10%：安全対策…設備・作業環境の安全対策/5S活動
■5%：技術研修…機械、電気、制御（PLC）に関わる技術トレーニングを受講
■5%：その他…メンテナンス計画/デザインレビュー
　
【取り扱う設備の一例（FCによって異なる）】
■Amazon Robotics（商品棚を持ち上げて移動させるロボット）
■荷合わせシステム（同じお客様が注文された商品を一つにまとめる設備）
■パッキング設備（荷合わせした商品をまとめて梱包する設備）
■仕分け設備（宛先ラベルの印刷と貼り付けを行い、配送方面毎に仕分けを行う設備）

A day in the life
【教育・研修】
・入社後は、体系化された教育プログラムに沿って、安全ルール、保全作業、専門的な技術習得を進めていただきます。集合研修も用意しており、少数のグループで座学だけでなく実務研修を行っていただきます。同時に所属拠点ではOJTを通してより実践的な業務スキルを習得いただきます。充実した技術研修プログラムとマンツーマンのサポート体制により、未経験の方でも着実にスキルアップできる環境を整えていますので、不明点の確認や相談がしやすい環境となります。
　
【働き方】
本ポジションは、シフト勤務になり「3交代・早番専属・夜勤専属」のシフトパターンがあります。
シフトパターンは希望を考慮の上、決定させていただきますが、将来的なシフト勤務形態の変更の可能性もございます。
＜シフト例＞※シフトは拠点によって異なります。
　・3交代 　6:00-15:00, 14:30-23:30, 23:00-8:00
　・早番専属　6:00-15:00
　・夜勤専属　23:00-8:00
シフトは1か月以上前に事前に周知される為、休暇の計画が立てやすく、チームで調整することで休暇が取得しやすい環境です。
退勤時間には次の班が出勤し引継ぎを行う為、残業時間が少なく、急な呼び出しも発生しません。
公共交通機関が運行しない時間帯でも交代勤務に出社できるようお願いいたします。
（自家用車・自転車は可（入社前に要申請・許可制）、バイク・タクシーは不可）

- オーナーシップ：自ら課題を見つけ、責任を持って解決できる。
- 探究心と向上心：いままで経験がない業務においても新しい知識を学ぶことに意欲的で、何事にも前向きに取組むことができる。
- チームワーク：リスペクトを持ってメンバーと協働でき、報連相ができる。
- 機敏性と柔軟性：変化する状況や環境に機敏、且つ柔軟に対応できる。
- インクルーシブなカルチャーへの貢献や多様性に富んだグループで働くことに対して前向きであること。

jobs.description:
External job description
Our WW Operations network delivers millions of packages and smiles to Amazon customers every day. We are looking for motivated, customer-focused individuals who want to join our team as an Area Manager. In this role, you will lead a team of hourly workforce and will be responsible for engaging your team during their shifts to maintain the highest levels of safety, quality, attendance, and performance. You will also play a key role in maintaining our customer expectations to ensure customer orders are delivered at the right time, to the right location.
Our Operation’s workflow has three major components: First mile - where the product is housed and ready for order; Middle mile - where the order is hauled to your area; and Last mile - when the product is delivered to the customer’s door. Please note that all workflows have slight building variations, but one thing is constant: our vision and dedication to the customer.

Our fulfillment network launches new Operations sites every year, providing various opportunities for your professional growth. We hire Area Managers based on location preference and the business’ current openings.


Key job responsibilities
Key Responsibilities and Job Elements:


Support, mentor, and motivate your hourly workforce

Manage safety, quality, productivity, and customer delivery promises

Collaborate with all support teams including Safety, Engineering, Loss Prevention, Quality Assurance, Human Resources to develop plans to meet business objectives

Lift up to 49 pounds and frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach

Stand/walk for up to 12 hours during shifts

Work in an environment where the noise level varies and can be loud

Work in an environment that is subject to variable temperatures and weather (delivery stations include outside loading departments)

Continuously climb and descend stairs (applies to sites with stairs)
